summary: A solid soundboard recording with Phil front‑and‑center in the mix, highlighted by a soaring Sugaree jam, powerful High Time and energetic Alabama opening.
review: The show is widely praised for its high‑energy second set—especially the expansive Sugaree jam that flows into Estimated > Eyes, the immortal Deal, and one of the last West Coast performances of High Time.  While the soundboard tape isn’t pristine (Phil’s bass dominates and there are a few rough edges), it captures the band’s tight playing and memorable moments, making it a worthwhile addition for any Deadhead.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and highlight many standout songs; the recording is good but not flawless, leading to a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Phil’s bass is very high in the mix ("Phil zone"), some roughness and occasional flubs noted, but overall clear enough to enjoy the performance.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set serves as a warm‑up, while the second set builds to a fever pitch with a crushing Music Never Stopped → Sugaree transition and sustained jams that keep the energy high before closing with a strong Deal.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s vocals are praised (especially on Sugaree) but he has a few flubs during Alabama; overall his playing is energetic.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass sits very high in the mix (“Phil zone”), described as blowing chunks off the ceiling—great for Phil fans.
band_member_comments.Bob: Brief nod to Bob’s classic Dead philosophy; no specific performance comments.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) receives positive remarks, noted as a solid force comparable to the Keith era.
band_member_comments.Drums: Post‑drum improvisations after Sugaree and space sections are highlighted as dreamy and well executed.
